title: SolarEdge Technologies (SEDG) Q3 2023 Earnings Call Transcript
text: SolarEdge Q3 2023 Earnings Call Highlights. Key Financial Results: Revenue: 725.3 million, down 27% from Q2 and 13% year-over-year. Solar segment revenue: 676.4 million (29% decline from prior quarter). Gross margin: 19.7% GAAP, 20.8% non-GAAP (down substantially from 32%+ prior quarter). Net loss: 61.2 million GAAP, 31 million non-GAAP. The Inventory Crisis: Management highlighted an unexpected market downturn driven by distributor inventory buildup. CEO Zvi Lando explained: market demand began to slow in the third quarter, and distributors began to experience financial challenges. The company accommodated significant order cancellations and delays from European distributors, particularly affecting battery shipments (down to 121 MWh from 269 MWh prior quarter). Forward Guidance: Q4 2023: Revenue guidance of 300-350 million with gross margins of 5-8%. Normalized outlook: Management projects stabilized revenues of 600-700 million quarterly post-inventory correction, with gross margins targeting 30-32% (including 500 basis points from IRA tax credits). Operational Actions: Discontinued Mexico manufacturing operations. Reduced China capacity. Ramping U.S. production targeting 50,000 energy hub inverters quarterly. Exiting light commercial vehicle e-mobility business. Authorized 300 million share repurchase program.
